V-M-060.purpose: Verify MTProto admin abuse alerts, operator review states, and confirmation-safe enforcement actions.

verification-levels.module-local: Alert model/service tests prove dedupe, state transitions, severity mapping, and safe payloads.
verification-levels.wave-level: Run with V-M-056 to prove high/critical abuse signals create alerts and with V-M-047/V-M-026 to prove actions are audited.
verification-levels.phase-level: Admin UI cannot close until open alert badges, alert list, review drawer, and confirmation actions are visible on mobile.
verification-levels.phase-79-level: No alert creation path may enforce access changes; only explicit admin-confirmed action routes may disable a proxy or apply a TTL IP block.

module-checks.check-1: Only high and critical abuse signals create operator alerts by default; low/medium remain stored as observe-only signals and are hidden from the operator UI by M-058.
module-checks.check-2: Dedupe key prevents repeated scheduler runs from flooding the admin inbox.
module-checks.check-3: Acknowledge and resolve state transitions record admin id and timestamps without changing runtime access.
module-checks.check-4: Disable proxy delegates to M-047 revoke and marks the alert action outcome after confirmation.
module-checks.check-5: TTL IP block refuses untrusted or stale IP evidence and requires double confirmation.
module-checks.check-6: Reviewed disable/resolve/IP-block actions move alerts out of the open inbox and preserve them for archive review.

phase-79-checks.check-1: Creating or deduping an alert does not change assignment status, revoke proxy credentials, update runtime block state, or resolve the alert automatically.
phase-79-checks.check-2: Proxy disable remains available only through an admin-confirmed route and affects only the selected assignment.
phase-79-checks.check-3: TTL IP block remains available only through an admin-confirmed route with trusted M-061 client-IP evidence and explicit TTL.
phase-79-checks.check-4: Acknowledge and resolve remain review/archival actions and do not perform hidden disable or hidden IP-block side effects.
phase-79-checks.check-5: Audit payloads remain safe: no proxy secrets, full tg links, promotion tag values, raw private keys, or copied raw IP payloads.

scenarios.scenario-1: High or critical abuse signals create exactly one open admin alert per dedupe key.
scenarios.scenario-2: Repeated scanner runs update the existing alert instead of flooding the inbox.
scenarios.scenario-3: Admin can acknowledge and resolve alerts without changing runtime access.
scenarios.scenario-4: Disable proxy action requires confirmation, revokes only the selected assignment, and writes an audit-safe event.
scenarios.scenario-5: TTL IP block action requires double confirmation and refuses to run without trusted M-061 client IP evidence.
scenarios.scenario-6: Alert payloads do not contain tg links, proxy secrets, runtime tokens, private keys, or copied raw IP strings.
scenarios.scenario-7: After a confirmed IP block, the alert status is resolved and action metadata is available to the archive.
scenarios.scenario-8: Hard MTProto abuse creates an open admin alert while the user proxy remains usable until the administrator chooses a confirmed action.
scenarios.scenario-9: Normal promo-sharing activity produces no open hard-abuse alert and no automatic disable or IP-block state.

commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_admin_alerts.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_analytics_api.py -v
python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/verification/V-M-060.xml docs/modules/M-060.xml docs/plans/Phase-43.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml

redaction-rules.rule-1: Alert list payloads may include assignment id, user id, user email under admin policy, signal type, metric, threshold, severity, and timestamps.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Alert payloads must not copy raw IP strings, tg links, proxy secrets, runtime tokens, or private keys.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Audit details must reference IP observation ids or hash prefixes, not decrypted raw IP strings.

acceptance.item-1: Admin alert inbox shows open high/critical abuse signals dynamically.
acceptance.item-2: Alert actions never auto-punish without explicit admin confirmation.
acceptance.item-3: Audit records contain safe identifiers and action reasons, not raw secrets or copied raw IP payloads.

V-M-060.evidence: pass-local 2026-06-06: alert lifecycle, dedupe, acknowledge/resolve, hard-abuse handoff, mobile-IP false-positive guard, stale-IP block rejection, recorded TTL block with resolved archive state, admin API action routes, frontend-admin build, Phase-43 static smoke, and Phase-79 no-auto-enforcement checks passed.

phase-79-verification-design.commands: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_admin_alerts.py tests/test_mtproto_analytics_service.py -v; node scripts/phase79-cyber-title-and-mtproto-promo-safe-smoke.mjs
phase-79-verification-design.acceptance: Alert creation, acknowledgement, dedupe, and archive movement remain advisory until explicit admin-confirmed disable-proxy or TTL IP-block actions run.
phase-79-verification-design.evidence: PASS local 2026-06-06: backend MTProto admin-alert and analytics tests prove alert creation/review does not auto-disable assignments or create blocked IP rows.
